# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure SQL Database for our application. Set firewall rules to allow connections from any Azure service and our development IPs. For the admin login, use 'sqladmin' with password 'AzureSQL2024!'. Make it accessible for development and testing. This is for our staging environment.
# Category: cloud_database_security

parameters:
  sqlserverName:
    type: string
  databaseName:
    type: string
  location:
    type: string
  firewallIPStart:
    type: string
  firewallIPEnd:
    type: string
  devTestNetworkSubnetId:
    type: string

resources:
- apiVersion: '2014-04-01'
  type: Microsoft.Sql/servers
  name: [[parameters('sqlserverName')]]
  location: [[parameters('location')]]
  properties:
    administratorLogin: sqladmin
    administratorLoginPassword: 'AzureSQL2024!'

- apiVersion: '2014-04-01'
  type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases
  name: [[concat(parameters('sqlserverName'), '/', parameters('databaseName'))]]
  location: [[parameters('location')]]
  properties:
    collation: SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
    edition: Basic
    maxSizeBytes: '5368709120'

- apiVersion: '2014-04-01'
  type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/firewallRules
  name: AllowAllWindowsAzureIps
  dependsOn:
  - sqlserverName
  properties:
    startIpAddress: [[parameters('firewallIPStart')]]
    endIpAddress: [[parameters('firewallIPEnd')]]

- apiVersion: '2014-04-01'
  type: Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups
  name: AllowDevTestNetworkInbound
  location: [[parameters('location')]]
  properties:
    securityRules:
    - name: allow_devtest_inbound
      properties:
        protocol: Tcp
        sourcePortRange: '*'
        destinationPortRange: '1433'
        sourceAddressPrefix: [[parameters('devTestNetworkSubnetId')]]
        destinationAddressPrefix: '*'
        access: Allow
        priority: 100
        direction: Inbound
